code_contracts_inspected: native structured-output request and transport parser

historical_prompt_embedded_transport.interpretation: The zero aggregate combines transport, exact schema, exact target, placeholder metadata, and task gates; it is not a JSON-capability verdict.

placeholder_metadata.interpretation: Placeholder text preservation and the declared placeholder inventory are separate axes.

output_budget.classification_rule_required: Treat explicit length or output_tokens >= configured cap as a length candidate.

positional_const_p4.result: HTTP 400 before generation on all four models
positional_const_p4.repair: Generic exactly-25-item schema plus post-generation ID/order validation

normalization.semantic_fidelity_definition: byte-exact normalized_text equality to semantic gold or a reference candidate
normalization.placeholder_rule: placeholder sequence in generated text must equal target sequence and declared metadata sequence
normalization.ordinal_scores: punctuation/casing and disfluency are 2 only on exact text equality, otherwise 0
normalization.accepted: all hard gates, thresholds, and ordinals pass

structural_overlay.behavior: non-normalization rows only test whether raw output parses as an object and always set accepted=false
structural_overlay.limit: This is evidence-preserving structural accounting, not semantic scoring.

factual_blocks_validator.schema: closed minimal shape validation, not full JSON Schema Draft validation

interpretations: Native schema binding improves structural compliance but does not guarantee raw serialization, semantic correctness, metadata correctness, or bounded generation.
Fenced JSON is a recoverable serialization defect for application runtime, but must remain separately logged from raw native-schema success.
The current normalization scorer is suitable for exact-reference conformance, not for broad semantic quality judgments.
The generic-schema P4 repair is the strongest tested architecture: keep request-specific identities out of the runtime grammar and validate them after generation.

recommended_runtime_contract.transport: Native strict JSON Schema when available; prompt-embedded schema is instruction text only and must not be labeled native structured output.
excluded_from_model_output: placeholder inventory duplicated as model-authored metadata

ordered_steps: Reject transport errors, missing final text, reasoning content, and empty output.
Classify length when the native reason is length/max_output_tokens or output-token usage reaches the configured cap.
Parse raw JSON first; optionally extract exactly one full fenced JSON document and record transport=fenced_recovered.
Validate the complete closed schema with a full JSON Schema validator in production.
Compare returned block IDs to application-owned expected IDs for exact set, count, uniqueness, and order.
Require non-empty block text and allowed status values.
Validate chunk-only boundaries, protected names/numbers/dates/URLs/commands/placeholders, unsupported additions, omissions, and runaway repetition.
Keep semantic review and exact-reference score as separate fields; never infer semantic failure from wrapper or metadata failure.
Accept only if every transport, schema, business, boundary, protected-value, and semantic safety gate passes.
post_generation_validator.fenced_policy: Recoverable only when one fence fully contains one JSON document with no surrounding prose; preserve raw_json=false and recovered_json=true.

one_retry_policy.retry_change: Use the same source and semantic instructions, compact native generic schema, and an explicit terse correction naming only the failed structural gate.
one_retry_policy.rule: A retry is a distinct attempt and never overwrites or reclassifies the first result.

fallback_policy.on_structural_failure_after_retry: Return the original source blocks unchanged, preserving application-owned IDs; mark fallback_original.
fallback_policy.on_semantic_or_safety_failure: Do not retry; return original source blocks unchanged and mark the precise safety failure.
fallback_policy.on_partial_validity: Fail the whole requested batch closed unless the caller explicitly supports per-block partial results and preserves exact ordering.
never: silently strip fences and call the response native-raw success

hypotheses: Removing model-authored input digests and duplicated placeholder inventories should reduce false rejection without reducing safety because the application can compute both deterministically.
A compact generic blocks schema should remain more robust at P4 than request-specific positional const grammars, but schema-complexity limits outside the tested 25-item case remain unknown.
unresolved_evidence_gaps: No controlled product-shaped plain-versus-native-JSON A/B holds the same approximately 23k-token prefix and current chunk constant.
No block-preserving end-to-end merge result validates early, middle, and late real chunks.
No retained execution demonstrates the proposed one-retry and fallback policy end to end.
Semantic completeness is based on direct private review, not exhaustive source-unit alignment or audio truth.
The current factual-block validator performs minimal shape checks rather than full JSON Schema Draft validation.
The safe upper bound for generic schema complexity and P4 across other block counts is unknown.

private_artifact_classes: 60 repeated native structured-output raw final texts
two extended E4B/M05 boundary raw outputs and envelopes
